source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Laceration injury from wire wheel grinder
actions_taken: Injured person evaluated by onboard medic
Injured person transported to Terrebonne General Medical Center in Houma, LA
event.summary: On 27 May 2021 at approximately 1415 hours, an employee of Eni US Operating Co. Inc. sustained a laceration to the thumb while using a grinder with a wire wheel to remove gasket material during compressor bearing replacement on platform MC 773 A (Devils Tower). The wire wheel caught and struck the employee's thumb, creating a small laceration requiring three sutures. The incident was attributed to improper PPE (non-cut-resistant gloves) and an incomplete Job Safety and Environmental Analysis (JSEA) that failed to identify the grinder hazard and required control measures.
recommendations: Ensure JSEA covers all tools and equipment used in job tasks
Require cut-resistant gloves for tasks involving sharp or abrasive tools
Ensure JSEA identifies all hazards and assigns appropriate risk-assessment metrics
Implement sufficient safeguards to reduce risk to ALARP

threats.name: Wire wheel catches and strikes hand/thumb
Inadequate PPE protection against cutting hazard

consequences.name: Laceration to thumb requiring medical treatment
Evacuation of injured person from platform
Lost work time and operational disruption

controls.name: Job Safety and Environmental Analysis (JSEA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and required control measures before work begins

supporting_text: The JSEA did not cover any use of a grinder with a wire wheel in any of the job steps.
The JSEA did not cover use of the grinder and the IP did not use cut resistant gloves.
Neither the addition of a tool / equipment (grinder with a wire wheel) or specific job steps to conduct a task (remove gasket material on a part) were captured on the JSA provided to the BSEE NOD.

controls.name: Cut-resistant gloves (PPE)
controls.barrier_role: Protect hand and thumb from cutting hazards
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment for hand protection

supporting_text: although the IP was wearing gloves, the gloves wear not cut resistant type gloves.
Improper PPE - Gloves worn by IP did not provide adequate protection against cuts and abrasions.

controls.name: Onboard medical evaluation and treatment
controls.barrier_role: Assess injury severity and provide immediate medical care

supporting_text: The IP was seen by the onboard medic who determined further medical evaluation by a medical clinic onshore was needed.
The IP was seen by the platform medic who determined to send the IP in for further evaluation at Terrebonne General Medical Center in Houma, LA.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
